2026-04-23T06:42:16.0211438Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0211973Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0212395Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0212758Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0213257Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0213494Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0213660Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0213849Z [36;1m[0m

2026-04-23T06:42:16.0214255Z [36;1m{[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0214459Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_api_key=${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0214741Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-23T06:42:16.0214998Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-23T06:46:44.2257101Z   Looking at these commits, the new ServiceNetworking provisioning package stands out as the most significant from a documentation angle, since it represents a new addition to the SDK rather than maintenance or migration work.

2026-04-23T06:46:44.2269746Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2270424Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning..., repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2271354Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2271588Z ◆ Now let me analyze the gaps:
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2271968Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2274198Z   1. The README has a "Create a Traffic Controller" example, but the code block uses `C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic` - this is a snippet placeholder. However, the code already appears to be manually pasted in the README rather than being pulled by the snippet tool. This is actually a documentation issue - the README has the code inlined rather than using the snippet system.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2276675Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2277534Z   2. The test file DOES have `#region Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic` and `#endregion` markers, which is correct for the snippet tool.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2278489Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2279132Z   3. The README has `## Authenticate the Client` section but it's EMPTY - no content about authentication.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2279874Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2280155Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2281104Z   4. The `## Next steps` section is also completely empty, and there's no `samples/` directory for the package either.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2282517Z   5. The `## Key concepts` section is minimal - just mentions declarative style and azd.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2283205Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2285598Z   I should look at a canonical Provisioning README to see what the expected structure looks like. Looking at the README content more carefully, I see there's actual code between the snippet fences rather than just an empty placeholder, which suggests the snippet tool hasn't been run yet or the code was pasted manually. I need to check what happens after running the snippet update process.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2288048Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2291432Z   Actually, examining the README more closely, I can see the code is already inlined in the snippet tags. The real gaps are clearer now: the `## Authenticate the Client` section is completely empty, `## Next steps` has no content, and the `## Key concepts` section doesn't describe the specific ServiceNetworking resources like TrafficController, Frontend, Association, and SecurityPolicy. Plus, there's no mention of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` in the examples even though the CHANGELOG indicates it was added.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2294882Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2295740Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/keyvault/Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault..., repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2296584Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2297581Z ◆ Looking at the canonical Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault README, it has the same structure as the new ServiceNetworking README - both have:
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2298678Z   - Empty `## Authenticate the Client` section
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2299167Z   - Empty `## Next steps` section
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2299535Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2300729Z   So these seem to be "template gaps" that are consistent across provisioning packages. The ServiceNetworking README actually follows the same pattern as the KeyVault one, including the same gaps.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2302676Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2302971Z   However, looking at the differences:
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2303450Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2304406Z   1. The ServiceNetworking README only documents `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation` in its example.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2305450Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2305693Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2306742Z   2. The CHANGELOG mentions `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` was added, but this resource isn't documented in the README or covered by any test snippets.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2307856Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2308690Z   These are actual documentation gaps specific to the new package - the security policy resource exists but lacks both examples and test coverage.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2309616Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2313476Z   Let me check the generated resource file to see what properties `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` exposes, and I'm also noticing that the README snippets are inline rather than placeholders, which suggests the snippet tooling either hasn't run yet or the code was manually inserted. Looking at the test file more closely, the snippet region is actually in a helper method rather than a `[Test]` method itself, which might not align with the snippet tool's expectations for test methods. I'm looking at the `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` resource to understand its structure and what documentation would be needed to properly showcase it.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2317151Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2318031Z ✓ github-get_file_contents(owner: Azure, path: sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning..., repo: azure-sdk-for-net)
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2318742Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2319151Z ◆ Now I have a clear picture. Let me summarize the documentation gaps:
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2319686Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2322127Z   1. **`ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` resource is not documented** - This is a newly added resource (`Microsoft.ServiceNetworking/trafficControllers/securityPolicies`) mentioned in the CHANGELOG but absent from the README examples. It represents WAF security policy configuration for Application Gateway for Containers.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2324277Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2326311Z   2. **`## Authenticate the Client` section is empty** - There's a heading but no content. However, this is consistent with the canonical `Azure.Provisioning.KeyVault` README, which also has an empty Authenticate section. So this might be an acceptable pattern for Provisioning packages (since provisioning packages don't directly authenticate to service endpoints in the same way).
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2328487Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2328741Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2329301Z   3. **`## Next steps` section is empty** - Also consistent with KeyVault provisioning README.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2329939Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2331318Z   4. **No sample file in a `samples/` directory** - While the test has the snippet, there's no separate `samples/` folder. However, looking at other Provisioning packages, this seems to also be consistent.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2332799Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2334043Z   5. **The README's `Examples` section only shows `TrafficController`, `TrafficControllerFrontend`, and `TrafficControllerAssociation`** - missing an example of `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy`.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2335401Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2336185Z   6. **No `[Test]` attribute on the snippet method** - The snippet method lacks this attribute, which other provisioning packages typically include.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2337063Z   
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2338461Z   The main issue is that `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` is listed as a key addition in the CHANGELOG but has no README example or test coverage, while the documentation only covers the other three resources. I'm going to file this now.
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2339931Z 
2026-04-23T06:46:44.2342242Z ◆ I have enough context to file the issue. The new `Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking` package (PR #58483) is missing documentation for the `ApplicationGatewayForContainersSecurityPolicy` resource — it's listed in the CHANGELOG as a key feature but absent from the README examples and has no test snippet.

Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-23T06:46:44.4545939Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-23T06:43:28.568693128Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"# Azure Provisioning ServiceNetworking client library for .NET\n\nAzure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking simplifies declarative resource provisioning in .NET.\n\n## Getting started\n\n### Install the package\n\nInstall the client library for .NET with [NuGet](https://www.nuget.org/ ):\n\n```dotnetcli\ndotnet add package Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking --prerelease\n```\n\n### Prerequisites\n\n\u003e You must have an [Azure subscription](https://azure.microsoft.com/free/dotnet/).\n\n### Authenticate the Client\n\n## Key concepts\n\nThis library allows you to specify your infrastructure in a declarative style using dotnet. You can then use azd to deploy your infrastructure to Azure directly without needing to write or maintain bicep or arm templates.\n\n## Examples\n\n### Create a Traffic Controller\n\nThis example demonstrates how to create an Azure Application Gateway for Containers (Traffic Controller).\n\n```C# Snippet:ServiceNetworkingBasic\nInfrastructure infra = new();\n\nTrafficController controller =\n    new(nameof(controller), TrafficController.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\n    {\n        Tags = { [\"environment\"] = \"test\" },\n    };\ninfra.Add(controller);\n\nTrafficControllerFrontend frontend =\n    new(nameof(frontend), TrafficControllerFrontend.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\n    {\n        Parent = controller,\n    };\ninfra.Add(frontend);\n\nTrafficControllerAssociation association =\n    new(nameof(association), TrafficControllerAssociation.ResourceVersions.V2025_01_01)\n    {\n        Parent = controller,\n        AssociationType = TrafficControllerAssociationType.Subnets,\n        SubnetId = new ResourceIdentifier(\"/subscriptions/00000000-0000-0000-0000-000000000000/resourceGroups/myRg/providers/Microsoft.Network/virtualNetworks/myVnet/subnets/mySubnet\"),\n    };\ninfra.Add(association);\n```\n\n## Troubleshooting\n\n-   File an issue via [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues).\n-   Check [previous questions](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/azure+.net) or ask new ones on Stack Overflow using Azure and .NET tags.\n\n## Next steps\n\n## Contributing\n\nFor details on contributing to this repository, see the [contributing\nguide][cg].\n\nThis project welcomes contributions and suggestions. Most contributions\nrequire you to agree to a Contributor License Agreement (CLA) declaring\nthat you have the right to, and actually do, grant us the rights to use\nyour contribution. For details, visit \u003chttps://cla.microsoft.com\u003e.\n\nWhen you submit a pull request, a CLA-bot will automatically determine\nwhether you need to provide a CLA and decorate the PR appropriately\n(for example, label, comment). Follow the instructions provided by the\nbot. You'll only need to do this action once across all repositories\nusing our CLA.\n\nThis project has adopted the [Microsoft Open Source Code of Conduct][coc]. For\nmore information, see the [Code of Conduct FAQ][coc_faq] or contact\n\u003copencode@microsoft.com\u003e with any other questions or comments.\n\n\u003c!-- LINKS --\u003e\n[cg]: https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/resourcemanager/Azure.ResourceManager/docs/CONTRIBUTING.md\n[coc]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/\n[coc_faq]: https://opensource.microsoft.com/codeofconduct/faq/\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/servicenetworking/Azure.Provisioning.ServiceNetworking/README.md"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
